reasoning: Critical correctness bugs prevent query execution (0 rows returned vs expected 4). Zone map parsing reads wrong memory layout, and dictionary encoding indexing needs verification. Must fix both correctness issues immediately. After fixes, pursue high-impact performance optimizations (SIMD vectorization, branch-free filtering) to close gap with DuckDB baseline (~87ms).
focus_areas: Fix zone map parsing to read 2x int32 + 2x int64 layout (24 bytes per entry)
Verify and correct dictionary encoding index base (1-indexed vs 0-indexed)
Implement AVX512 SIMD vectorization for arithmetic operations (system supports avx512f)
Apply branch-free filtering for highly selective date predicate (98.8% selectivity)
strategy_notes: CORRECTNESS FIRST: Both critical fixes (zone map parsing + dictionary encoding) must be implemented before any performance work. The zone map bug causes empty valid_ranges by misinterpreting int64_t offsets as int32_t pairs. Fix the memory layout parsing in load_zone_map() and verify dictionary indexing. After correctness is restored, aggressively pursue performance: (1) AVX512 vectorization for the arithmetic-heavy inner loop (process 8 doubles per instruction), (2) Branch-free filtering to eliminate ~59M branch mispredictions on the 98.8% selective predicate, (3) Consider removing zone map overhead entirely for such high selectivity. Target: Match or beat DuckDB's ~87ms baseline. System has AVX512 support - exploit it fully.
notes: Priority order: critical_fixes [0,1] must be done first and together. Then performance_optimizations [2,3] (SIMD + branch-free) which work synergistically. With 2 iterations remaining, be aggressive on vectorization - the hardware supports it and the inner loop is arithmetic-intensive.
